title: Leptodesmia bojeriana
taxonomy: Leptodesmia bojeriana is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Madagascar and belongs to the genus Leptodesmia. The classification of Leptodesmia bojeriana includes its kingdom as Plantae, its clade as Angiosperms, and its order as Fabales. This plant is further classified into the family Fabaceae and the genus Leptodesmia. Leptodesmia bojeriana's classification also includes its species as Leptodesmia bojeriana.

intro: Leptodesmia bojeriana, also known as "Ma'o hau hele," is a flowering shrub native to the island of Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Leptodesmia bojeriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
